title: Wire ToolCache into tool execution pipeline
boundary: Tool result caching for read-only tools with write-side invalidation
consumer_purpose: Reduce redundant tool executions within a session by caching results of cacheable read tools, expiring via TTL, and invalidating on writes
claim_atoms: 1. Tools with cacheable:true get cached results on repeated calls with same args within TTL window. 2. Write/modify tools clear the read cache on successful execution. 3. Cache misses are indistinguishable from bypass — no new error modes.
content: Final plan with all subagent feedback incorporated. Key architectural decisions: (1) Effect.serviceOption avoids signature changes to wrap()/define(), (2) full cache clear on writes avoids metadata normalization complexity, (3) file_paths convention in ExecuteResult.metadata (not Context.metadata()).
revision_reason: Incorporating load-bearer + blueprint findings: serviceOption for zero-sig-change, full cache clear for simplification, file_paths in result metadata not context
